mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return probability drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, severe labor market deterioration signals collapsing consumer spending power and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable consumer prices indicates robust real purchasing power, which directly fuels corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL rises faster than RSAFS, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days specifically during post-crisis recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes noise with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle expansions or when industrial production growth decouples from equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and wage growth, which consistently drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with equity returns with Rank IC below 0.02, or positive return probability drops below 52 percent across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally with positive returns in more than 60 percent of cases despite CPI spikes, or the threshold fails to predict returns during periods of accommodative monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic demand, which amplifies corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when housing market dynamics diverge from broader economic trends, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines for two consecutive months, manufacturing contraction signals impending earnings downgrades and supply bottlenecks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive industrial production declines, or the signal fails to predict short-term moves with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling windows.
